rationale: Both correctly land on 'duplicate of #4808,' matching the thread, but Y also surfaces the #4809 triage-issue reference that the maintainer explicitly pointed to, while X omits it entirely. X asserts 'already fixed on main' with high confidence yet its own findings note PR #4808 is CLOSED (not merged), an unresolved contradiction it never addresses; Y instead flags this exact gap (mergedAt null) as an open question needing confirmation, which is the more epistemically honest move on a genuinely unverified point. Y is also more complete, proposing a concrete regression-test coverage fix tied to why the bug slipped through, whereas X's answer stops at 'already fixed, no further action.'
